basic_details.job_title: BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online Link Activated for 100 Junior Telecom Officer Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL), Nigam Karyalay, Bharti Anubhag, New Delhi – 110001
basic_details.post_name: Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR] – Direct Recruitment from Open Market
basic_details.department: Bharti Anubhag (Recruitment Section), BSNL Corporate Office, New Delhi
basic_details.total_vacancies: 100 (Provisional; BSNL reserves right to change the number of posts)
basic_details.job_type: Regular / Permanent; Probation period: 2 years; Bond: Rs. 6 Lakhs for 5 years of service
basic_details.application_mode: Online only (via BSNL External Exam portal: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in)
basic_details.short_information: BSNL is recruiting 100 Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) posts through Direct Recruitment from open market, with B.E./B.Tech. (relevant disciplines) or M.Sc. (Electronics/CS) qualification, last date 03 July 2026.

important_dates.age calculated as on: Closing date of receipt of application (03-07-2026)
important_dates.admit_card_date: Available via BSNL website using username & password (not by post/e-mail)

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay the prescribed examination fee through online mode only (Internet Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card) on the BSNL External Exam portal (www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in). Fee once paid will NOT be refunded under any circumstances nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination or selection. Additionally, a challenge/grievance window fee of Rs. 400/- per question/answer key is charged post-examination (non-refundable).

age_limit.details: Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and not exceed 30 years as on the closing date of receipt of application (03-07-2026). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved / EWS / OBC (CL) candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Central Government guidelines.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (03-07-2026). Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. The applicant must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision. The qualification must have been completed as on the closing date of receipt of application from a University recognized under UGC Act, 1956.
qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor of Engineering Degree or equivalent from a Central Govt. / State Govt. recognized Institution/University in any of the following disciplines: (1) Telecommunications, (2) Electronics, (3) Radio, (4) Computer, (5) Electrical, (6) Information Technology (IT), (7) Instrumentation OR M.Sc. (Electronics) / M.Sc. (Computer Science). Note: Candidates possessing higher qualifications viz. M.Tech. in the prescribed disciplines are also eligible.

vacancy_details.Category: Deaf or Hard of Hearing (HI) – Category (b)
vacancy_details.Description: Hard of Hearing / Deaf as defined under RPwD Act 2016, with not less than 40% disability as certified by certifying authority. Benefit of reservation may be given to PwBDs with Permanent Disability Certificates only.

vacancy_details.Category: Locomotor Disability including Cerebral Palsy, leprosy cured, dwarfism, acid attack victims and muscular dystrophy – Category (c)
vacancy_details.Description: Locomotor Disability as defined under RPwD Act 2016, with not less than 40% disability. Forms for Certificate of Disability may be same as prescribed under RPwD Act 2016 / RPwD Rules 2017 as amended from time to time.

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Locomotor Disability as defined under RPwD Act 2016, with not less than 40% disability. Forms for Certificate of Disability may be same as prescribed under RPwD Act 2016 / RPwD Rules 2017 as amended from time to time.

salary_details.pay_scale: IDA Pay Scale [E-1]: Rs. 16,400 – Rs. 40,500/- (2nd PRC pay scale w.e.f. 01.01.2007)

selection_process: Section Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
I Engineering Stream Section – I 50 200 3 Hours (Single shift, all sections combined)
III General Ability Test Section – III 20 80

Selection will be based on Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT). All candidates will be given a score based on their performance in the CBT. A final merit list will be prepared based on total marks obtained across all three sections. Selected candidates will go for document verification and medical examination. Appearance in the final merit list does not confer any right for appointment — a final offer letter will be issued only after successful completion of all pre-appointment formalities. BSNL reserves the right to cancel/restrict/enlarge/modify/alter the recruitment/selection process at any stage without notice.
Stage 1 – Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT)

Marking Scheme: For each correct answer: +4 Marks. For each wrong answer: -1 Mark (Negative Marking). The standard of engineering papers will be that of Engineering Degree Examination of an Indian University. In the General Ability Test
special attention will be paid to assess the candidate's capacity for general awareness
at the level expected of an Engineering Graduate. If the examination is held in multiple sessions

normalization of marks will be adopted. Minimum Qualifying Marks: For UR/OBC (CL) candidates — minimum 40% in each section and minimum 50% overall across all three sections combined. For SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PwBD/EWS candidates — minimum 35% in each section and minimum 45% overall. Candidates not meeting sectional or overall minimum qualifying marks will not be included in the merit list. The final merit list will be prepared based on total marks in all three sections combined
subject to minimum qualifying marks criteria being met.

Candidates in the declared merit list will be called for document verification. Date and venue will be intimated in due course. All original certificates along with self-attested photocopies must be produced at the time of document verification.

Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to their passing the Company's Medical Examination as per standards laid down by BSNL. Candidates who are already employed elsewhere must resign from their current organization only after being declared medically fit. Details will be provided on BSNL website www.bsnl.co.in.

318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263

pattern_table.Key Subtopics: Structure and properties of Electronic Engineering materials; Conductors, Semiconductors and Insulators; Magnetic, Ferroelectric, Piezoelectric, Ceramic, Optical and Superconducting materials; Passive components and characteristics – Resistors, Capacitors, Inductors; Ferrites, Quartz crystal, Ceramic resonators; Electromagnetic and Electromechanical components.

pattern_table.Topic: 2. Physical Electronics, Electron Devices and ICs
pattern_table.Key Subtopics: Electrons and holes in semiconductors; Carrier Statistics; Mechanics of current flow in a semi-conductor; Hall effect; Junction theory; Different types of diodes and their characteristics; Bipolar Junction transistor; Field effect transistors; Power switching devices like SCRs, CTOs, power MOSFETs; Basics of ICs-bipolar, MOS and CMOS types; Basics of Opto Electronics.

Network analysis techniques; Network theorem; Transient and steady state sinusoidal response; Transmission criteria – delay and rise time, Elmore's and other definitions; Effect of cascading; Elements of network synthesis.
Transmission lines – basic theory, standing waves, matching applications, microstrip lines; Basics of waveguides and resonators; Elements of antenna theory.

Basic concepts, standards and error analysis; Measurements of basic electrical quantities and parameters; Electronic measuring instruments and their principles of working – analog and digital, comparison, characteristics, applications; Transducers; Electronic measurements of non-electrical quantities like temperature, pressure, humidity; Basics of telemetry for industrial use.

Power Semiconductor devices; Thyristor; Power transistor; MOSFETs; Characteristics and operation; AC to DC convertors; 1-Phase and 3-phase DC to DC Convertors; AC regulators; Thyristor controlled reactors; Switched capacitor networks; Inverters – Single-phase and 3-phase; Pulse width modulation; Sinusoidal modulation with uniform sampling; Switched mode power supplies.

Transistor biasing and stabilization; Small Signal analysis; Power amplifiers; Frequency response; Wide band techniques; Feedback amplifiers; Tuned amplifiers; Oscillators; Rectifiers and power supplies; Operational Amplifier; Other linear integrated circuits and applications; Pulse shaping circuits and waveform generators.

Transistor as a switching element; Boolean algebra; Simplification of Boolean functions; Karnaugh Map and applications; IC Logic gates and their characteristics; IC logic families – DTL, TTL, ECL, NMOS, PMOS and CMOS gates and their comparison; Combinational logic circuits; Half adder, full adder; Digital Compartor; Multiplexer; Demultiplexer; ROM and their applications; Flip-flops – R-S, J-K, D and T types; Different types of counters and registers; Waveform generators; A/D and D/A converters; Semiconductor memories.

Transient and steady state response of control systems; Effect of feedback on stability and sensitivity; Root locus techniques; Frequency response analysis; Concepts of gain and phase margins; Constant-M and Constant-N Nichol's Chart; Approximation of transient response from Constant-N Nichol's Chart; Approximation of transient response from closed loop frequency response; Design of Control Systems; Compensators; Industrial controllers.

Basic information theory; Modulation and detection in analogue and digital systems; Sampling and data reconstruction; Quantization & Coding; Time division and frequency division multiplexing; Equalisation; Optical Communication – in free space & fibre optic; Propagation of signals at HF, VHF, UHF and microwave frequency; Satellite communication.

Microwave Tubes and solid state devices; Microwave generation and amplifiers; Waveguides and other Microwave Components and Circuits; Microstrip circuits; Microwave antennas; Microwave Measurements; MASERS; LASERS; Microwave Propagation; Microwave Communication Systems – terrestrial and satellite based.

Number Systems; Data representation; Programming; Elements of a high level programming language PASCAL/C; Use of basic data structures; Fundamentals of computer architecture processor design; Control unit design; Memory organization; I/O System Organization; Personal computers and their typical uses.
Microprocessor architecture – Instruction set and simple assembly language programming; Interfacing for memory and I/O; Applications of Microprocessors in Telecommunications and power system.

General English comprehension and understanding; Current Events and General Knowledge; Everyday observation and experience in scientific aspects as may be expected of an educated person; Events and developments in Telecommunications; History of India; Geography. Questions will be of a nature that can be answered without special study by an educated person.

exam_pattern.details: The examination is a Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT) of 3 hours duration consisting of three sections. Sections I and II cover Engineering Stream subjects at B.E./B.Tech. degree level, and Section III covers General Ability. The detailed syllabus topic-wise is given below. | Section I – Engineering Stream (Topics) | Section II – Engineering Stream (Topics) | Section III – General Ability Test | Examination Centre Cities (27 Cities – ANNEXURE-C) | The examination will be held in 27 cities/centres across India. Candidates must choose only one Examination City/Centre at the time of registration. The exam centre may be any of the 27 cities or a nearby city depending upon number of candidates. The 27 cities are: Port Blair, Vijayawada, Guwahati, Patna, Chennai, Raipur, Ahmedabad, Ambala, Shimla, Jammu, Ranchi, Bangalore, Thiruvananthapuram, Kolkata, Bhopal, Mumbai, Shillong, Dimapur, New Delhi, Bhubaneshwar, Chandigarh, Jaipur, Gangtok, Hyderabad, Lucknow, Meerut, Dehradun. No request for change of examination centre will be entertained after submission. | BSNL JTO Syllabus and Exam Pattern 2026

physical_eligibility.details: The competition level for BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high, as only 100 posts are available across 21 Circles for an All-India open market recruitment targeting Engineering graduates in Electronics/Telecom/CS/IT and related disciplines. Karnataka (26 seats), Kerala (15 seats), and Maharashtra (16 seats) will attract the highest number of serious applicants since they have the most vacancies and are also home to a large pool of engineering graduates. Circles like Rajasthan, Sikkim, Tamilnadu, and Uttarakhand with only 1 seat each will be the most competitive on a per-seat basis, where even a fraction of a mark can decide selection. | The 480-mark, 3-hour CBT with sectional minimum qualifying marks makes it critical to maintain balance across all three sections — candidates who neglect the General Ability section or fail to meet the per-section cut-off (40%/35%) will be eliminated regardless of their total score. The Rs. 2,000 exam fee and the mandatory 5-year bond (Rs. 6 Lakhs) will filter out casual applicants, leaving mostly serious engineering graduates. The Circle preference system rewards candidates who opt for more circles, as it gives BSNL flexibility to allocate based on merit and availability.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only through the BSNL External Exam portal (www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in). No manual/paper application will be entertained. Candidates should keep the following ready before starting the registration process: valid mobile number and email ID for OTP, scanned photograph, signature, Class 10th certificate, Degree Certificate, Consolidated Mark Sheet, Photo ID proof, Category/Disability Certificate (if applicable).
Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:
Recent passport-size colour photograph with white background in JPG/JPEG format (20kb – 50kb only)
Signature in JPG/JPEG format (10kb – 20kb only)
Class 10th Certificate (for verification of Date of Birth)
Degree Certificate of Essential Qualification
Consolidated Mark Sheet / transcript of essential qualification for all semesters or years
Photo identity proof (Aadhaar Card, Passport, Driving License, Voter ID Card)
Valid proof of reservation category as applicable (SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD)
Medical Certificate for PwBD (issued by Govt. Medical Board in prescribed format)
Documents to be Produced at Document Verification / Appointment:
Admit Card (downloaded from www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in using username & password) along with valid photo identity proof in original
All original educational qualification certificates and mark sheets
Caste Certificate in prescribed Central Government format (for SC/ST candidates)
OBC (NCL) Certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026 in prescribed Central Government format (for OBC NCL candidates)
Income and Asset Certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026 in prescribed format (for EWS candidates)
Disability Certificate issued by Govt. Medical Board in prescribed format (for PwBD candidates)
"No Objection Certificate" from present employer (for candidates employed in Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies)
Date of Birth proof (Matriculation / Secondary School Leaving Certificate or equivalent)
Apply Online at: https://www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in

For queries: Contact BSNL Corporate Office, New Delhi at [email protected]. BSNL does not seek payments of any kind other than the registration fee and post-exam grievance fee. Candidates are advised not to respond to unscrupulous advertisements; check authenticity only on www.bsnl.co.in.
important_instructions: Both internal BSNL employees and external (open market) candidates fulfilling the prescribed eligibility conditions are eligible to apply. JTO is a Circle Cadre post; candidates will be allotted a Circle depending on vacancies, their preference, and merit. Options once exercised shall be final — change of Circle afterwards will not be allowed. Candidates are strongly advised to select all 21 Circles in order of preference to maximise the possibility of selection. Selecting a limited number of Circles restricts candidature only to those opted Circles. All selected candidates must execute a Bond of Rs. 6 Lakhs to serve BSNL for at least 5 years from the date of appointment as Junior Telecom Officer. The admit card will be available only for download from the BSNL website — it will NOT be sent by post or email. Issuance of admit card does not confer any right for appointment. After closing of registration period, an Editing Window will be opened for 7 days — candidates can edit selective fields only. No further editing will be allowed after the editing period. Candidates applying for UR vacancy can also be SC/ST/EWS/OBC/Ex-Serviceman/BSNL employees subject to meeting all general eligibility standards — category relaxation in such cases cannot be availed. Candidature is liable to be rejected at any stage if any information provided is found false or not in conformity with the eligibility criteria mentioned in the advertisement. No request for withdrawal of candidature will be entertained. Any dispute will be subject to the courts/tribunals having jurisdiction at New Delhi. Canvassing in any form or influencing officials related to the selection/recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of the candidate. Candidates who have a living spouse and have entered into another marriage, or who have married a person with a living spouse, are not eligible for appointment. BSNL reserves the right to modify/amend any terms and conditions of this recruitment at any point of time. Dispute, if any, shall be within the jurisdiction of Delhi only. BSNL Management reserves the right to cancel the recruitment exercise fully/partially at any stage at its discretion without notice. BSNL does not seek payments of any kind other than the registration fee and post-exam grievance fee — candidates are advised to beware of fraudulent advertisements.

_labels.notification_pdf: Download BSNL JTO Direct Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF
_labels.apply_online: Apply Online for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026

faq.question: Q1. What is BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 is a direct recruitment drive for Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR] posts in Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited from the open market. A total of 100 vacancies will be filled across various BSNL Circles under Advt. No. BSNLCO-11/12(11)/1/2026-RECTT-CO.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in BSNL JTO (Telecom) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 100 JTO (Telecom) vacancies distributed circle‑wise across BSNL’s territorial units. Major circles with higher intake include Karnataka (26 posts), Kerala (15 posts), Maharashtra (16 posts) and Madhya Pradesh (10 posts), while other circles have 1–6 posts each as per Table‑A.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online registration starts from 4 June 2026 at 10:00 AM and ends on 3 July 2026 at 10:00 AM. Candidates can edit their applications from 4 July 2026 (10:00 AM) to 11 July 2026 (10:00 AM), and the Computer Based Test (CBT) is tentatively scheduled in August 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility (qualification) for BSNL JTO (Telecom) 2026?
faq.answer: For the Telecom stream, candidates must have a B.E./B.Tech degree (or equivalent) in Telecommunications, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, IT or Instrumentation from a recognized institution. Alternatively, M.Sc. (Electronics) or M.Sc. (Computer Science) degree holders are also eligible, and those with M.Tech in the prescribed disciplines may also apply.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The basic age limit is 20 to 30 years as on the closing date of application. Relaxations apply as follows: OBC (NCL) up to 33 years, SC/ST up to 35 years, PwBD up to 40–45 years, and further relaxations for Ex‑Servicemen and BSNL employees as per Central Government and BSNL rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary and bond condition for BSNL JTO (Telecom) posts?
faq.answer: Selected JTO (Telecom) candidates will be appointed in the IDA Pay Scale E‑1: ₹16,400 – ₹40,500 (2nd PRC, w.e.f. 01.01.2007) with usual allowances as admissible. They will be on a two‑year probation and must execute a service bond of ₹6 lakhs to serve BSNL for a minimum period of 5 years from the date of appointment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the exam pattern for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through a Computer Based Test (CBT) of 3 hours, containing 120 objective questions for 480 marks. Section I: Engineering Stream–I – 50 questions, 200 marks Section II: Engineering Stream–II – 50 questions, 200 marks Section III: General Ability Test – 20 questions, 80 marks Each correct answer carries +4 marks, and each wrong answer attracts –1 mark (negative marking).

faq.question: Q8. What are the minimum qualifying marks in BSNL JTO CBT 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must secure at least 40% marks in each section individually. Overall, UR/OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates must obtain 50% aggregate, while SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PwBD/EWS candidates need at least 45% aggregate across all three sections to be considered for merit list preparation.

faq.question: Q9. What is the application fee for BSNL JTO (Telecom) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The examination fee is ₹2,000 for UR/OBC/EWS candidates and ₹1,000 for SC/ST/PwBD candidates. The fee is non‑refundable and must be paid online only via Internet Banking, Credit Card or Debit Card on www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible Indian nationals must apply online only at www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in during the registration window. Candidates should register with a valid mobile number and email ID, upload scanned photo and signature, fill academic and personal details, select up to 21 Circle preferences, pay the online fee, and finally print and retain the registered application for future reference. An editing window from 4–11 July 2026 allows limited corrections, but Circle preferences and some critical fields may not be editable.

title: BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online Link Activated for 100 Junior Telecom Officer Posts
